displayTitle: 2025-08-20 - Primary Production Committee - Briefing on new agricultural compounds
description: Thursday 21 August 2025 | Primary Production Committee Briefing on new agricultural compounds Hearing of Evidence Minrock Mineralisation Ltd - Owen Jennings - John Third
descriptionSummary: Briefing on new agricultural compounds. Minrock Mineralisation Ltd. Owen Jennings

chunks.text: Discuss with you mineralization and your mineral presentation. Yeah. So these guys, he's a plant physiologist. He does a lot of work in tissue culture and has got a deep understanding of the needs for cells to survive and thrive when you're doing that fine tissue culture work. Um, and they said, look, all of the literature deals with 11 to 15 minerals.

There's 212 in the periodic table. What are they there for? And then started to say, well, look, it's all good and well for the plants, because we sort of understand the process of having to have soluble minerals that go through the roots. What do the microbes need?

Is it the same and is it different? And guess what it's not. And guess what? The microbes are deficient in critical minerals that are absolutely essential for microbial reproduction. So you'll get a very low level of microbes surviving in the soil that are native there, but they're deficient in these critical minerals that they need for reproduction.

And when it's supplied, it's like Viagra for microbes. They multiply dramatically. Um, they're attracted to the roots of the plant, and they cluster around and attach themselves to the roots. If we look at this picture here that looks like the dreadlocks, that's what happens under this system where the microbes have formed a fiberglass test tube for the wonder of a term around the root.

The microbes, uh, you know, the numbers are in the billions in the soil, but if we start talking about bacteria, for instance, they've got a life cycle of eight to 10 hours. And so now we've got a fiberglass test tube holding a perfectly mineralized soup of dead and dying bacteria.

And so they're providing all of the minerals for the plant in a balanced and perfect way that they can just dip into. But it goes beyond that. It's beyond the simple NPK type minerals and includes, um, complex biochemicals. So hormones, growth stimulants, antibiotics, antivirals.

And so the other feature with this is that in all cases, they require no artificial fertilizers, no pesticides, and they more than double the yield. So the other graph that's worth turning to, if you want a quick look, is the, one of the crop yields
